tactical_shape: Jesse Marsch's side with early gusto, lots of pressure and substitutes used to replenish the frontline
Broadly carried on where they left off, with promising moves fizzling in the final phase
Triple substitution on the hour to refresh the attack
Needed a late box presence; Larin was told to get into the box and score
standout_players: Cyle Larin: supersub, scored with his first touch and rescued the point
Jonathan David: missed a golden opportunity / sitter in the first half but helped create the equaliser
Richie Laryea: had a goal-bound effort superbly blocked onto the crossbar by Kolasinac; later had a sidefoot effort saved/cleared after slick interchange
Tani Oluwaseyi: replaced before Larin came on; Nikola Katic headed off the line to deny him
home_read.momentum: First point at a World Cup on home soil; a spirited response that left Canada believing, though Marsch said improvements are needed to beat Qatar.

tactical_shape: Started with Jovo Lukic, Ermedin Demirovic and Amar Memic up top / in attack, with Edin Dzeko short of fitness on the sidelines
Scored from a training-ground routine and then defended under sustained Canada pressure
Held a more reactive shape after taking the lead, with clearances and goal-line defending
standout_players: Jovo Lukic: first international goal, header from a set-piece routine
Sead Kolasinac: produced an extraordinary block to stop Laryea and prevent a goal, influential in defence
Nikola Katic: headed off the line to deny Oluwaseyi
Edin Dzeko: short of fitness, on the sidelines in a warmup bib encouraging teammates
away_read.momentum: A creditable away draw in a hostile opening-night atmosphere; Barbarez will take pride that Bosnia did not succumb to the pressure.
